name: Cirrhosis of liver
description: Cirrhosis of liver — advanced scarring of the liver, all causes; one of the APACHE IV chronic health conditions.

en.name: Cirrhosis of liver
en.shortDescription: Cirrhosis of liver — advanced scarring of the liver, all causes; one of the APACHE IV chronic health conditions.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Cirrhosis is advanced, diffuse scarring (fibrosis) of the liver with nodular regeneration, the common end-stage of many chronic liver diseases. In intensive care it is a major comorbidity affecting coagulation, fluid balance, infection risk, drug handling and prognosis. Cirrhosis is one of the chronic health items in the APACHE IV severity model.

## Scope

The set is anchored on the SNOMED concept *4064161 / 19943007 — Cirrhosis of liver* with all descendants included, so it captures cirrhosis of **all causes** (biliary, viral, cardiac, non-alcoholic, alcoholic, cryptogenic, etc.), including advanced and decompensated forms.

## Mapping Notes

- All aetiologies of cirrhosis map here. Alcoholic cirrhosis maps both here and to the Alcoholic liver disease set (the two variables answer different questions).
- Hepatic failure (loss of liver function) is captured by its own set; a cirrhotic patient who develops hepatic failure maps to both.
